understanding.project_summary: This project provides an automated developer-documentation engine (AutoDocX) that scans a local Python project, extracts code structure and FastAPI routes, generates LLM-based narrative understanding, and produces standalone HTML documentation. It exposes a FastAPI backend (app.api.project_routes) with endpoints to scan projects, compare scans, generate/return documentation, and accept optional runtime/tooling context (screenshots and notes) returned via a Streamlit UI client (ui.streamlit_app).
understanding.architecture_overview: AutoDocX is layered into: 1) scanning & parsing (app.services.project_scanner and app.services.code_parser) which enumerate files, parse Python ASTs and compute stable content hashes; 2) analysis & context (app.services.project_analyzer and app.services.project_context_builder) which build project-level graphs, routes, module references and compact LLM-ready context; 3) LLM orchestration (app.services.project_understanding_service, app.services.runtime_context_analyzer and app.services.llm.*) which select a provider, issue prompts (text or multimodal), validate and retry responses; 4) persistent storage (app.services.scan_storage, app.services.understanding_storage, app.services.document_storage) which save scans, understandings, HTML documents and optional runtime assets; 5) presentation & API (app.services.document_builder builds HTML; app.api.project_routes exposes REST endpoints; ui.streamlit_app is a client helper/UI). Code moves from the scanner -> analyzer -> context builder -> LLM service -> understanding merge -> document builder -> document storage, with API handlers coordinating these steps and optional runtime analysis using vision-capable LLM calls for screenshots.

execution_flow.title: Scan project files
execution_flow.description: Call ProjectScanner.scan_project to enumerate files, ignore configured folders, compute file hashes and parse Python files into AST-based structures using PythonCodeParser.

execution_flow.title: Analyze and build project context
execution_flow.description: ProjectAnalyzer.analyze_project collects modules, routes, internal dependencies and statistics; ProjectContextBuilder.build_context compacts that analysis into detailed or LLM-optimized context.

execution_flow.title: Generate structured understanding with LLM
execution_flow.description: ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding builds a prompt (app.services.llm.prompts), selects an LLM provider via LLMProviderFactory, requests JSON output, validates required fields and retries once if malformed.

execution_flow.title: Merge code reference data and build HTML documentation
execution_flow.description: Merge reliable parser/analyzer data into LLM output, then DocumentBuilder.build_html renders developer-ready HTML and DocumentStorage.save_document writes HTML and metadata to disk.

execution_flow.title: Optional runtime/tooling analysis
execution_flow.description: When runtime context and screenshots are provided, runtime_context_analyzer compacts project context, sends multimodal prompts to a vision-capable provider, and attaches the runtime_understanding into the document metadata and rendered HTML.

execution_flow.title: Compare scans and version documents
execution_flow.description: ScanComparator.compare_scans compares saved scans to detect documentation-relevant changes (files, symbols, routes, dependencies). If changes exist, the system produces a new understanding and document version; otherwise it returns the existing documentation metadata.

6. Returns the existing document when there

- class methods

- return annotations
- docstrings
- decorators
- FastAPI routes
- source previews
- content hashes
- syntax errors

constants.name: PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX, a professional developer documentation engine.

You receive deterministic project context extracted from source code.
The context may include modules, classes, functions, method signatures,
FastAPI routes, dependencies, docstrings, and project statistics.

Your job is to generate clean developer documentation metadata.

Important rules:

1. Do not invent files, functions, classes, APIs, databases, services,
   dependencies, parameters, return values, or behavior.
2. Keep module names, function names, class names, method names, and API
   paths exactly as supplied.
3. Write like professional developer docs, not like an audit report.
4. Do not write phrases like "Confirmed", "Reasonable inference",
   "Based on the context", or "It appears".
5. If something is unclear, write a short neutral sentence such as:
   "The provided scan does not expose this detail."
6. Do not include Markdown code fences.
7. Do not include API keys, credentials, secrets, or environment values.
8. Return exactly one valid JSON object.
9. Do not return text before or after the JSON object.

The JSON object must follow this exact structure:

{
  "project_summary": "string",
  "architecture_overview": "string",
  "execution_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",

constants.name: RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX Runtime Context Analyzer.

Your job is to analyze a software project using:
1. Parsed project/code scan context.
2. User-written runtime/tooling notes.
3. Screenshots attached by the user.

You must understand what the project does outside the codebase:
- how it runs
- what tools are involved
- what commands are used
- what services are connected
- how UI/backend/API/workflows interact
- what the screenshots prove
- what a developer should understand from this evidence

You MUST return only valid JSON.
Do not use markdown.
Do not wrap the response in ```json.

Return this exact JSON structure:

{
  "runtime_summary": "string",
  "tooling_stack": [
    {
      "tool": "string",
      "purpose": "string",
      "evidence": "string"
    }
  ],
  "runtime_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "evidence": "string"
    }
  ],
  "screenshot_insights": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "what_it_shows": "string",
      "why_it_matters": "string"
    }
  ],
  "operational_notes": [
    "string"
  ],
  "risks_or_gaps": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "severity": "low|medium|high"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Use the screenshots as evidence, not decoration.
- Use the user notes as hints, but correct and improve them.
- Use project scan context to connect runtime behavior to actual code.
- Do not invent external systems that are not supported by code, notes, or screenshots.
- If a screenshot shows terminal commands, ports, URLs, services, or errors, explain them.
- Keep the output developer-facing and practical.
'

risks_and_gaps.title: No authentication or authorization on API endpoints
risks_and_gaps.description: The scanned code and routes expose no authentication or JWT/ACL enforcement. Endpoints that run scans and call external LLMs (costly operations) are unauthenticated in the detected surface.

risks_and_gaps.title: LLM API keys and provider configuration required at runtime
risks_and_gaps.description: LLM provider, API key and model are read from environment via app.core.config.get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model; missing or incorrect .env values will raise errors. There is no documented safe key storage or secret rotation mechanism in code.

risks_and_gaps.title: Limited observability and metrics
risks_and_gaps.description: The code contains print statements for retry logging but lacks structured observability (metrics, traces, structured logs) and centralized error monitoring hooks for production readiness.

risks_and_gaps.title: Retry and rate-limit handling for external LLMs
risks_and_gaps.description: The LLM call patterns include a single retry on parse failure; there is no exponential backoff, rate-limit handling, or configurable retry policy documented in the API surface.

risks_and_gaps.title: Persistence and backup considerations
risks_and_gaps.description: Scans, understandings and documents are stored on the local workspace path; there is no documented support for configurable remote storage, backups or retention policy.

risks_and_gaps.title: Input validation and error mapping
risks_and_gaps.description: Several internal ValueError-to-HTTP conversions exist, but broader validation, consistent error codes and client-facing error schemas are limited; client UI code includes basic fallbacks but a consistent contract could be improved.

risks_and_gaps.title: Testing and CI coverage not exposed
risks_and_gaps.description: The project provides many deterministic behaviors (parsing, comparisons, storage) but the provided context does not include unit/integration tests or CI configuration.

risks_and_gaps.title: Deployment and scalability
risks_and_gaps.description: No deployment manifests, containerization details or horizontal-scaling patterns are present. LLM calls and file I/O may require resource planning when used at scale.

recommended_document_sections.title: Quickstart and CLI/GUI usage
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Show how to run the API, use the Streamlit UI, and execute core flows (scan, sync, sync-with-context) with example commands and expected outputs.

recommended_document_sections.title: Architecture and data flow
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Diagram layers (scanner, analyzer, context builder, LLM service, storage, builder, API) and describe how data flows through each component and files on disk.

recommended_document_sections.title: API Reference
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Endpoint descriptions, request/response schemas (models in app.models.project), status codes and example requests for every route exposed by app.api.project_routes.

recommended_document_sections.title: LLM configuration and provider guide
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Explain get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model usage, supported providers (openai, anthropic), model selection, required .env variables and security recommendations for API keys.

recommended_document_sections.title: Storage, file layout and backups
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document workspace directory layout (scans, understandings, documents), file naming conventions, asset batch handling and recommendations for backing up or switching to remote storage.

recommended_document_sections.title: Runtime & Tooling Context (screenshots)
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guide on preparing context blocks and screenshots, how the system links screenshots to blocks, and what the RuntimeContextAnalyzer expects and returns.

recommended_document_sections.title: Error handling, retries and operational guidance
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Document failure modes, retry behavior for LLM calls, common errors (missing env keys, storage not writable), recommended alerting and mitigation steps.

recommended_document_sections.title: Testing and development
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Guidance for unit/integration testing the parser, analyzer, comparator and storage layers, plus example test cases and suggested CI checks.

recommended_document_sections.title: Extending providers and customization
recommended_document_sections.purpose: Instructions for adding new LLM providers, customizing prompts, changing HTML templates in DocumentBuilder, and tuning comparison criteria.

- FastAPI routes
- source previews

constants.name: PROJECT_UNDERSTANDING_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX, a professional developer documentation engine.

You receive deterministic project context extracted from source code.
The context may include modules, classes, functions, method signatures,
FastAPI routes, dependencies, docstrings, and project statistics.

Your job is to generate clean developer documentation metadata.

Important rules:

1. Do not invent files, functions, classes, APIs, databases, services,
   dependencies, parameters, return values, or behavior.
2. Keep module names, function names, class names, method names, and API
   paths exactly as supplied.
3. Write like professional developer docs, not like an audit report.
4. Do not write phrases like "Confirmed", "Reasonable inference",
   "Based on the context", or "It appears".
5. If something is unclear, write a short neutral sentence such as:
   "The provided scan does not expose this detail."
6. Do not include Markdown code fences.
7. Do not include API keys, credentials, secrets, or environment values.
8. Return exactly one valid JSON object.
9. Do not return text before or after the JSON object.

The JSON object must follow this exact structure:

{
  "project_summary": "string",
  "architecture_overview": "string",
  "execution_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "related_modules": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "module_responsibilities": [
    {
      "module": "string",
      "file": "string",
      "responsibility": "string",
      "important_symbols": ["string"]
    }
  ],
  "api_overview": [
    {
      "method": "string",
      "path": "string",
      "handler": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "key_dependencies": [
    {
      "source": "string",
      "target": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ],
  "risks_and_gaps": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "severity": "low | medium | high"
    }
  ],
  "recommended_document_sections": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "purpose": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Style requirements:

- project_summary should be 1 to 2 concise paragraphs.
- architecture_overview should explain the application layers and how
  code moves through them.
- execution_flow should describe runtime flow in practical developer terms.
- module_responsibilities should be short and specific.
- api_overview should explain what each endpoint is used for.
- risks_and_gaps should focus on real engineering gaps such as
  authentication, error handling, retries, persistence, testing,
  configuration, observability, and deployment readiness.
- recommended_document_sections should suggest useful developer docs,
  not generic business report sections.
'.strip()

constants.name: RUNTIME_CONTEXT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
'
You are AutoDocX Runtime Context Analyzer.

Your job is to analyze a software project using:
1. Parsed project/code scan context.
2. User-written runtime/tooling notes.
3. Screenshots attached by the user.

You must understand what the project does outside the codebase:
- how it runs
- what tools are involved
- what commands are used
- what services are connected
- how UI/backend/API/workflows interact
- what the screenshots prove
- what a developer should understand from this evidence

You MUST return only valid JSON.
Do not use markdown.
Do not wrap the response in ```json.

Return this exact JSON structure:

{
  "runtime_summary": "string",
  "tooling_stack": [
    {
      "tool": "string",
      "purpose": "string",
      "evidence": "string"
    }
  ],
  "runtime_flow": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "evidence": "string"
    }
  ],
  "screenshot_insights": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "what_it_shows": "string",
      "why_it_matters": "string"
    }
  ],
  "operational_notes": [
    "string"
  ],
  "risks_or_gaps": [
    {
      "title": "string",
      "description": "string",
      "severity": "low|medium|high"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Use the screenshots as evidence, not decoration.
- Use the user notes as hints, but correct and improve them.
- Use project scan context to connect runtime behavior to actual code.
- Do not invent external systems that are not supported by code, notes, or screenshots.
- If a screenshot shows terminal commands, ports, URLs, services, or errors, explain them.
- Keep the output developer-facing and practical.
'
